title: Coherence and staleness
description: Across layers, end-to-end staleness is additive and the slowest-to-purge copy sets the floor.
body: Use when multiple caches or replicas hold copies of the same data. Coherence is the guarantee that all copies converge to the latest value; staleness is how long a copy may lag. Independent TTLs across layers make end-to-end staleness additive, and a copy that cannot be purged sets the staleness floor. Do not assume invalidating one tier fixes all of them. Actions: model the worst case as the sum of un-purgeable layer TTLs. Validation signals include a converged read after update across every tier. Risk boundary: user-facing correctness depends on the slowest-to-converge layer.
